content.rendered:
“Dead Flowers” is as close as The Rolling Stones ever got to country music.

You could even argue they got all the way there: Classic Rock Magazine calls “Dead Flowers” the third-greatest country rock song of all time, in fact.

So when Bruce Springsteen joined Steve Earle on stage in early 1998 for a remarkably rare set of covers and Earle originals (every single one of the six songs Bruce played that night was a true one-off, never played by him before or since), “Dead Flowers” was perhaps the most appropriate cover possible for country rocker Earle and country-inspired rocker Springsteen to tackle together.

Here’s that one-time-only performance, from February 6, 1998 at Tradewinds in Sea Bright, New Jersey. Earle takes the lead vocal (it was his show, after all), but if you listen carefully you can easily hear Bruce in the background.

It’s too bad Bruce didn’t take more of a foreground role on this one; “Dead Flowers” cries out for a true Springsteen cover sometime down the road.

Dead Flowers
First performed: February 6, 1998 (Sea Bright, NJ)
Last performed: February 6, 1998 (Sea Bright, NJ)

© January 25, 2020

excerpt.rendered: One time only: Bruce joins Steve Earle for a faithful cover of The Rolling Stones’ country rock classic, “Dead Flowers.”

jetpack-related-posts.title: Sweet Virginia
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce and Steve Earle team up on a Rolling Stones classic "British hillbilly song."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Everybody’s Trying to Be My Baby
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joins Steve Earle on stage for a cover of a rock classic that goes much further back than you might think.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Cover Me, Steve Earle: State Trooper
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: Steve Earle has been covering Bruce since the outset of his career– and this ACL performance is one of his strongest.

jetpack-related-posts.title: Johnny Come Lately
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joins Steve Earle and the Dukes on "Johnny Come Lately," Earle's sibling song to "Born in the U.S.A."

jetpack-related-posts.title: Gimme Shelter
jetpack-related-posts.author: Ken
jetpack-related-posts.excerpt: One time only: Bruce joins Joe Grushecky and The Houserockers on a blistering cover of The Rolling Stones' anthem "Gimme Shelter."
